ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic dumpling class; no specific pork-and-chive boiled dumpling in soup entry exists. Closest parent class used as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers boiled dumplings broadly; no pork-and-chive-specific code exists. Closest parent class used as closest defensible anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for pork and chive boiled dumpling in soup; left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is 6 dumplings in clear broth, approximately 220 g total including soup, providing about 202 kcal, 10 g protein, 14.7 g fat, and 7.6 g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Pork and chives dumpling (6 pc, in soup) — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for pork and chives dumpling (6 pc, in soup), CR ID F06100026, with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for pork and chive boiled dumplings (FDC ID 2103705) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this item.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Wheat-Based Boiled Dumplings — analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entry for boiled wheat dumplings / pasta-type products
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for pork and chive boiled dumplings in soup. GI of 48 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for boiled wheat-flour dumplings and pasta-type products (GI range 40–55 for boiled wheat products). The thin wheat wrapper and protein-fat filling moderate glycaemic response. GL per serving calculated as: GI (48) × carbohydrate per serving (7.6 g) / 100 = 3.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 404 mg/100 g; a 220 g serving delivers approximately 889 mg sodium, representing 39% of the 2300 mg daily limit in a single dish.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should consume this dish sparingly, request reduced soy sauce and salt in preparation, avoid drinking the soup broth, and account for this dish's sodium contribution within a daily limit of 2300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate at 3.47 g/100 g with minimal sugar (0.07 g/100 g) and a low estimated GI of 48; protein at 4.53 g/100 g moderates postprandial glycaemic response.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes may include this dish in moderate portions (6 dumplings), monitoring total carbohydrate load from the serving (~7.6 g), avoiding sweet dipping sauces, and pairing with non-starchy vegetables to further blunt glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Fat at 6.67 g/100 g including estimated saturated fat of 2.3 g/100 g from pork filling, combined with high sodium at 404 mg/100 g, presents dual cardiovascular risk factors.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to 6 dumplings, avoid the soup broth to reduce sodium intake, choose lean pork or substitute with chicken or prawn filling where possible, and ensure this dish fits within a daily saturated fat target of less than 20 g.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork contains arachidonic acid (omega-6 fatty acid) which amplifies pro-inflammatory eicosanoid pathways; sodium at 404 mg/100 g may worsen joint-related tissue oedema.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should substitute pork filling with chicken, prawn, or tofu, request reduced soy sauce and salt in preparation, and limit soup broth consumption to minimise sodium-driven inflammatory exacerbation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pork and Chives Dumpling in Soup (6 pieces) is a traditional Chinese-style boiled dumpling dish served in clear broth, widely consumed in Singapore as a hawker centre staple and home-cooked meal. Each dumpling consists of a thin wheat-flour wrapper encasing a filling of minced pork and Chinese chives (jiucai), seasoned with soy sauce, sesame oil, and ginger. The dumplings are boiled until the wrappers become soft and slightly translucent, then served in a lightly salted clear broth. The texture is tender and yielding, with a savoury, mildly garlicky filling. Per 100 g, this dish provides 92 kcal, 4.53 g protein, 6.67 g fat, 3.47 g carbohydrate, 0.07 g sugar, 0.76 g dietary fibre, and 404 mg sodium. A standard serving of 6 dumplings with soup (approximately 220 g) delivers approximately 202 kcal and 889 mg sodium. The dish has a low estimated glycaemic index of 48, making it suitable for blood glucose management in moderate portions. However, the high sodium content (404 mg/100 g) makes it a concern for hypertension and cardiovascular disease patients, who should avoid drinking the broth and request reduced seasoning. The pork filling contributes arachidonic acid, which may amplify inflammatory pathways in rheumatoid arthritis patients. Culturally, boiled dumplings are associated with Chinese New Year and family gatherings, symbolising prosperity and togetherness. Also known as shui jiao (水饺) in Mandarin.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (CR ID F06100026) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central closest analogue. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous boiled wheat dumpling entries. All mandatory multilingual names present for Singaporean Chinese dish category.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient, and moisture data for this specific pork and chive boiled dumpling in soup preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F06100026) or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. Saturated fat (2.3 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 2103705 (pork and chive boiled dumplings) as the closest approved-source analogue; this is an adaptation and not an exact match. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for boiled wheat-flour dumpling and pasta-type products; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish exists in the approved GI database. The input dietary_tags included 'keto' which is not supported given carbohydrate content of 3.47 g/100 g in a wheat-wrapper product; this tag has been removed. The input dietary_tag 'low_calorie' is retained as 92 kcal/100 g is consistent with low-calorie classification. Halal status is marked false as pork is a primary ingredient.
